# Pipeline dials ‚Äî auto-generated manifest

Generated by `python -m tools.pipeline_dials` ‚Äî **do not edit by hand**. Every field of every pipeline config dataclass (plus the shapesnap graph tag tables), read from source so the values never drift. The narrative for *why* each dial exists lives in `docs/transit-tuning-log.md`.

## shapesnap (graph tag policy)

`shapesnap.graph tables`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `RAIL_INCLUDE` | `{"rail", "subway", "light_rail", "tram", "narrow_gauge", "funicular"}` |  | ‚Äî |
| `RAIL_EXCLUDE` | `{"abandoned", "razed", "disused", "proposed", "construction", "plat‚Ä¶` |  | ‚Äî |
| `RAIL_SERVICE_PENALTY` | `{"crossover": 1.75, "siding": 1.75, "spur": 1.75, "yard": 4.0}` |  | ‚Äî |
| `RAIL_SERVICE_DEFAULT` | `2.0` |  | unknown service=* values: conservative |
| `RAIL_USAGE_PENALTY` | `{"industrial": 2.0, "military": 2.0, "tourism": 2.0}` |  | ‚Äî |
| `NON_REGULAR_SERVICE_VALUES` | `("yard", "siding", "spur", "crossover")` |  | DISPLAY-GEOMETRY track set (regular-service predicate). A "regular-service" track is one a train in revenue service actually runs on for through travel ‚Äî the mainline. Non-running tracks (yards, sidings, spurs, crossovers; industrial/military/tourism usage) are PENALIZED-BUT-AVAILABLE in matching (trains do use crossovers to reverse at terminals ‚Äî see RAIL_SERVICE_PENALTY), but they must NOT be ground truth for DISPLAY geometry: a yard's fan of parallel tracks pulls a pair/family midline and a reconciliation snap toward track no service rides, and real transit diagrams ignore them. is_regular_service_track() is the single predicate for that filter (reconcile_offtrack_corridors, the pair/platform midline, and the track-fidelity ground truth). This is a display filter, NOT a matching change ‚Äî the classify_rail penalties above are untouched. |
| `NON_REGULAR_USAGE_VALUES` | `("industrial", "military", "tourism")` |  | ‚Äî |

## shapesnap (HMM matcher)

`MatchConfig`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `dense_radius` | `field(default_factory=lambda: {"rail": 50.0, "bus": 35.0, "ferry": ‚Ä¶` | dict | candidate search (meters) |
| `sparse_radius` | `field(default_factory=lambda: {"rail": 200.0, "bus": 100.0, "ferry"‚Ä¶` | dict | ‚Äî |
| `k_candidates` | `10` | int | ‚Äî |
| `resample_m` | `30.0` | float | regime A resampling |
| `max_obs` | `2000` | int | ‚Äî |
| `min_step_m` | `5.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `sigma_dense` | `15.0` | float | emission / transition |
| `sigma_sparse` | `30.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `beta_dense` | `30.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `beta_sparse` | `300.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `cutoff_factor` | `3.0` | float | bounded network search |
| `cutoff_slack_dense_m` | `150.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `cutoff_slack_sparse_m` | `1500.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `route_bonus_mult` | `0.5` | float | per-edge weight shaping |
| `uturn_penalty_m` | `200.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `uturn_cos` | `-0.866` | float | reversal = turn sharper than ~150¬∞ vertical disambiguation: where elevated and subway tracks stack (Lake St: the Lake 'L' runs directly ABOVE the Milwaukee-Dearborn subway) horizontal emissions tie, so candidates whose OSM route relations do NOT match the GTFS route pay this emission prior. Only active when the route has relation coverage among the pattern's candidates; layers without any match add a constant, which cannot change the decoded chain. |
| `relation_prior` | `0.35` | float | vertical disambiguation: where elevated and subway tracks stack (Lake St: the Lake 'L' runs directly ABOVE the Milwaukee-Dearborn subway) horizontal emissions tie, so candidates whose OSM route relations do NOT match the GTFS route pay this emission prior. Only active when the route has relation coverage among the pattern's candidates; layers without any match add a constant, which cannot change the decoded chain. |
| `foreign_identity_prior` | `6.0` | float | identity-tier hard prior: on patterns that identity-match (tier 2) somewhere, an edge decorated ONLY with other routes' identity relations (RouteMatcher.is_foreign) is near-impossible to ride. 6.0 > 0.5*(50/15)^2 ‚âà 5.6 (the max in-radius dense emission), so in a mixed layer an identity-matched track anywhere inside the radius always outranks a foreign-identity track hugging the shape (MTA R at Canal St: degenerate chords < 3 m from N/Q bridge tracks, 40+ m from the R's own Broadway local). |
| `foreign_excise_ext_m` | `30.0` | float | an excised foreign run swallows adjacent observations whose nearest identity-matched candidate is farther than this ‚Äî the shape is still describing the foreign corridor there (2√ósigma_dense) |
| `foreign_excise_stop_span_max_m` | `1500.0` | float | ...and widens to the ENCLOSING STOPS (the trusted anchors) when they bracket the run within this span: the excursion's on-corridor tails (a walk-back after a self-reversal, the chord home) are the same fabricated drawing, and stops are where service pins the shape again (MTA R: Canal St ‚Üí Prince St span, 967 m) |
| `gap_retry_radius_mult` | `2.0` | float | gap retry (dense): a break's gap is re-matched once with the candidate radius (and emission sigma, proportionally ‚Äî so the max in-radius emission cost 0.5*(r/sigma)^2 is invariant and the foreign_identity_prior dominance rule above still holds) widened by this factor before falling back to the original-shape bridge. Covers systematic agency-vs-OSM offsets just past the radius (4/5 Joralemon St Tunnel: 47-64 m off the tunnel way for ~420 m, rail radius 50 -> 100). <=1 disables and restores pure break+bridge. |
| `bridge_route_cutoff_factor` | `4.0` | float | graph-routed gap bridges (on-OSM policy): a break that survives the gap retry is routed through the OSM graph between the pinned anchor candidates before falling back to the original-geometry bridge ‚Äî same weighted cost model as decoding (class penalties, route- relation bonus on edge weights, turn restrictions + reversal penalties). The weighted-cost cutoff is cutoff_factor x the gap's along-reference length, floored at min_cutoff_m so junction- adjacent micro-gaps still get about an urban block of routing budget. Feasible -> splice (stats.gaps[].bridge_kind="graph"); infeasible (OSM genuinely disconnected there) -> agency bridge exactly as before ("agency"). cutoff_factor <= 0 disables routing and restores pure agency bridging. |
| `bridge_route_min_cutoff_m` | `300.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `sparse_rescue` | `True` | bool | sparse rescue (on-OSM policy): a dense pattern whose quality gates fail is re-matched once in the SPARSE regime (stop-sequence matching on the same graph) instead of returning agency geometry. The rescue is NOT gated on Fr√©chet-vs-agency ‚Äî the agency shape is exactly what failed (96 St: the MTA shape ends 103 m short of the stop while the OSM track passes 1.5 m from it) ‚Äî but on: - stop-snap: every stop within the sparse candidate radius of the output (evaluate_gates' stop gate), - length plausibility vs the stop-chain chord, bounds below, - no empty layers: every stop must hold >=1 candidate. A stop beyond radius of any way would be chord-bridged straight THROUGH its own coordinate, faking the stop-snap gate ‚Äî an empty layer is direct evidence OSM lacks the track there. Bounds: the chord is a lower bound on any real path (network >= straight line) but the OUTPUT can measure slightly under it when off-track stop coordinates project inward onto the track at terminals (96 St again) or zigzag stop placement inflates the chord, hence min 0.90 (vs the dense regime's 0.95); max 1.60 is the battle-tested sparse bound ‚Äî real detours (rivers, one-way loops) fit, while >1.6x chord signals wrong-corridor wandering. |
| `rescue_length_ratio_min` | `0.90` | float | ‚Äî |
| `rescue_length_ratio_max` | `1.60` | float | ‚Äî |
| `station_search_radius_m` | `120.0` | float | regime B extras |
| `name_bonus_weight` | `1.5` | float | ‚Äî |
| `station_pass_radius_m` | `25.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `station_pass_penalty` | `1.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `degenerate_min_points` | `4` | int | degenerate-shape detection (regime A -> B demotion) |
| `degenerate_min_len_ratio` | `0.5` | float | shape length vs stop-chain chord output |
| `simplify_m` | `1.0` | float | output |
| `gates` | `field(default_factory=GateConfig)` | GateConfig | ‚Äî |

## shapesnap (quality gates)

`GateConfig`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `coverage_tol_m` | `field(         default_factory=lambda: {"rail": 25.0, "bus": 15.0, ‚Ä¶` | dict | ‚Äî |
| `min_coverage` | `0.95` | float | ‚Äî |
| `max_frechet_m` | `100.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `length_ratio_min` | `0.95` | float | ‚Äî |
| `length_ratio_max` | `1.15` | float | ‚Äî |
| `sparse_length_ratio_min` | `0.95` | float | sparse regime: output vs stop-chain chord length (chord is a lower bound) |
| `sparse_length_ratio_max` | `1.60` | float | ‚Äî |
| `frechet_segmentize_m` | `20.0` | float | discrete Fr√©chet is vertex-coupled: densify both lines to a fixed spacing first or a long simplified segment dominates the metric |

## linegraph (corridors + bundling)

`WaygraphConfig`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `snap_tol_m` | `2.5` | float | shape->way identity tolerance (see module doc) |
| `sample_step_m` | `3.0` | float | shape densification step |
| `edge_member_frac` | `0.5` | float | covered fraction that claims an edge... |
| `edge_member_min_m` | `25.0` | float | ...or this much covered length |
| `offgraph_min_m` | `15.0` | float | unsnapped span that becomes explicit geometry |
| `terminal_trim_pad_m` | `5.0` | float | keep this much past covered track at termini connectivity repair: consecutive ridden edges that share no node are reconnected via the shortest way-graph path that hugs the shape ‚Äî switch ladders contain near-coincident micro edges (spur twins) and nearest-snap alone can pick the dead twin (CTA Pink at the Paulina Connector: two 1.1 m fragments 0 m apart) |
| `repair_max_detour_m` | `60.0` | float | connectivity repair: consecutive ridden edges that share no node are reconnected via the shortest way-graph path that hugs the shape ‚Äî switch ladders contain near-coincident micro edges (spur twins) and nearest-snap alone can pick the dead twin (CTA Pink at the Paulina Connector: two 1.1 m fragments 0 m apart) |
| `repair_shape_tol_m` | `15.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `dangle_max_m` | `8.0` | float | member micro-dangles this short are snap noise |
| `pair_gap_m` | `15.0` | float | merge 1: directional pair (identical route sets) |
| `family_gap_m` | `25.0` | float | merge 2: same colour family, different sets |
| `family_sustained_min_m` | `450.0` | float | a family co-run this long merges even when both corridors continue past the window (local/express diverging at a real fork ‚Äî the 7th Av 1 vs 2/3); kisses/crossings stay far below this |
| `cross_family_gap_m` | `18.0` | float | merge 3: cross-family proximity bundle. RAISED 10 -> 18 (round 19): a KISS is a transient V-shaped convergence, a BUNDLE is a STABLE parallel co-run ‚Äî the two are told apart by PROFILE (the gates below), not by a low gap minimum. At 10 m genuine parallels a bit farther apart under-bundled into two messy ropes (DeKalb: the orange B/D beside yellow N/Q/R/W down the Manhattan Bridge approach measure a stable ~13/17 m mean/max gap, sustained, 0.9 deg relative bearing, no mid-span crossing ‚Äî a textbook bundle that 10 m rejected). 18 clears DeKalb's 17.4 m max with margin; going to 22 additionally fused stacked express/ local track at the Queens Blvd interlocking (E,F over E,R) whose midline bowed the through-ribbon (junction exam), so 18 is the sweet spot. The profile gates keep kisses out at either value. |
| `cross_family_min_len_m` | `200.0` | float | ...sustained at least this long. LOWERED 450 -> 200 (round 21): the 450 m floor dropped genuine ~200-450 m parallels that render as two crowded independent lines (Jay St A/C+F, J/Z+M on the Williamsburg approach, the ~330 m near-coincident A/C beside B/D). The anti-kiss PROFILE gates (non-crossing, gap-stability frac/ratio, bearing) keep the kisses out at the shorter length ‚Äî a real kiss crosses or is a transient valley regardless of how short the floor is, so lowering the floor admits bundles, not kisses (Rector/Brooklyn-Bridge/Whitehall stay rejected by crosses/frac). |
| `cross_family_sustained_min_m` | `450.0` | float | a cross-family co-run this long BUNDLES even when BOTH corridors continue past the window (they are not co-terminal ‚Äî the window is mid-corridor and both diverge only at a real fork downstream: the 4/5 beside 2/3 for 1.25 km toward Nevins/Atlantic, A/C beside B/D ~945 m, F beside N/Q/R ~567 m). This is the cross-family analogue of family_sustained_min_m and the transitive- bundling mechanism: once E has merged into a trunk, F sustains parallel to that BUNDLE centerline and joins it here, collapsing a whole physical corridor into ONE multi-slot bundle. The full profile gates still apply, so a long non-crossing KISS (a wide neighbourhood with a brief valley) fails frac_below; a crossing fails non-crossing. |
| `cross_family_max_bearing_deg` | `20.0` | float | parallel, not crossing |
| `cross_family_min_frac_below` | `0.60` | float | the co-run must be SUSTAINED over its NEIGHBOURHOOD: at least this fraction of a context window (the merge window grown by cross_family_min_len_m/2 each side) sits below the gap threshold. The merge window itself is all-below by construction, so the context is what discriminates ‚Äî a kiss is a below-threshold VALLEY inside an above-threshold neighbourhood (Rector, Whitehall dip under only near closest approach); a bundle stays under across the whole context (DeKalb, Chicago's Lake leg -> ~1.0). |
| `cross_family_max_gap_ratio` | `6.0` | float | loose safety valve on the below-threshold gap_max/gap_mean: genuine bundles breathe a LOT (Chicago P+Red 4.1, the Lake leg 3.2 ‚Äî the gap dips to 0 at shared switches and rises to ~14 m between), so this only catches a pathological spike, never a real co-run. |
| `cross_family_cross_slack_m` | `40.0` | float | NON-CROSSING gate: the two corridor geometries must not INTERSECT in the window INTERIOR (endpoint convergence at a shared switch is fine ‚Äî intersections within this slack of a window end are ignored). A mid-span crossing (Rector 1 x R/W, Whitehall crossing tubes) is a kiss and never merges, however low its gap dips. |
| `merge_min_len_m` | `40.0` | float | merges 1-2: minimum sustained window |
| `merge_end_slack_m` | `40.0` | float | window may stop this short of a corridor end |
| `merge_max_bearing_deg` | `35.0` | float | merges 1-2 sanity (switch ladders bend) |
| `absorb_max_len_m` | `120.0` | float | ridden crossover absorbed into its bundle |
| `tail_collapse_m` | `8.0` | float | merge tails shorter than this join the node (small: a longitudinal collapse slides the junction along the track, and forks must stay within ~5 m of the real switch nodes) |
| `midline_step_m` | `5.0` | float | merged midline resample step window flap guard (Schmitt trigger): real 4-track spacing breathes across the gap threshold (the Sea Beach pair tiled into 5 windows); qualifying windows separated by a short dip coalesce, and a formed bundle only releases where the gap CLEARLY lets go ‚Äî beyond release_gap_mult x gap sustained for release_sustain_m |
| `window_dip_coalesce_m` | `200.0` | float | dips shorter than this never split |
| `release_gap_mult` | `1.5` | float | ‚Äî |
| `release_sustain_m` | `150.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `ease_len_m` | `100.0` | float | C1 merge-boundary easing: where a merged midline hands off to a continuing constituent track the centerline used to step sideways by half the pair gap; the midline now eases into the through track over this length (clamped to the window) with a smoothstep ‚Äî zero step, tangent-continuous. Applies to the midline and to cut tails of corridors CONTINUING past the boundary; diverging switch legs keep the short blend below (dragging a real diverging track 100 m sideways parallels it with the bundle ‚Äî the Culver portal lesson from the junction exam). |
| `blend_m` | `25.0` | float | endpoint retarget / diverging-leg blend |
| `cov_cut_margin_m` | `30.0` | float | partial mid-edge coverage: a route entering/leaving a long way edge partway (the FX express rejoining the Culver local mid-way) cuts the edge at its coverage boundary instead of claiming track it never rides; cuts closer than this to an edge end are snap noise |
| `ladder_contract_m` | `16.0` | float | junction-to-junction micro corridors (switch-ladder fragments) contract to a single node ‚Äî under half a merge gap, so forks stay within ~5 m of the switch |
| `station_sliver_m` | `18.0` | float | lg.merge_width_m: stations.py node sliver |

## lineorder (slot ordering)

`SolveConfig`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `exhaustive_max_space` | `500` | int | ‚Äî |
| `cpsat_time_limit` | `30.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `cpsat_workers` | `8` | int | ‚Äî |
| `anneal_iters` | `30_000` | int | ‚Äî |
| `anneal_t0` | `1000.0` | float | ‚Äî |
| `seed` | `0` | int | ‚Äî |
| `jobs` | `0` | int | 0 = auto (cpu count), 1 = sequential |

## segments (steady/transition + bands)

`SegmentConfig`

| dial | default | type | what it does |
|------|---------|------|--------------|
| `transition_len_m` | `60.0` | float | fixed ground length centred on the node |
| `gap_px` | `GAP_PX` | float | on-screen ribbon spacing |
| `densify_step_m` | `7.5` | float | max vertex spacing in transitions |
| `fillet_radius_factor` | `2.5` | float | min radius = max ribbon count * gap_px * this (m) |
| `straight_skip_deg` | `2.0` | float | corner turn below this => fillet no-op |
| `offset_eps_px` | `0.01` | float | offset delta below this => unchanged |
| `probe_dist_m` | `40.0` | float | shape-evidence probe distance from node |
| `evidence_tol_m` | `30.0` | float | shape-to-probe distance tolerance |
| `bands` | `DEFAULT_BANDS` | tuple | ((min_zoom, transition_len_m), ...) |
| `cusp_turn_deg` | `150.0` | float | vertex turn above this = reversal cusp |
| `cusp_window_m` | `20.0` | float | max cusp cluster span worth excising |
| `cusp_pad_m` | `7.5` | float | excision window padding past the cusp |
| `loop_window_m` | `100.0` | float | max corridor self-intersection loop arc worth excising (micro-artifacts only; genuine balloon loops stay) |
| `fillet_len_budget` | `1.1` | float | max emitted transition length as a factor of the site's transition length (mirrors the exam's C3 cap) |
| `track_snap_tol_m` | `18.0` | float | a steady corridor whose centerline strays past this from the real OSM track is reconciled back onto it (off-graph agency-bridged runs) ‚Äî well above any legit bundle-midline offset (NYC 4-track trunks measure <= ~12 m), so on-track geometry and chicago:l-v3 are never touched |
| `track_snap_vertex_m` | `8.0` | float | per-vertex off-track threshold in the way-snap fallback (project only the vertices genuinely off the track) |
| `track_snap_max_dist_m` | `120.0` | float | never snap onto a way farther than this (a corridor with no nearby track ‚Äî off-map, ferry ‚Äî is left as is rather than yanked onto a stranger) |
